incorrects.description: Student selected 60 (added pencils and markers instead of finding the difference)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: When we ask how many more, we compare. That means subtract, not add. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40 (chose the value for pencils without comparing)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: That's how many pencils were sold. But the question asks how many MORE. We need to find the difference. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. Subtract to compare.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15 (incorrect calculation or misread values)

params.description: Pencils bar (40) and Markers bar (20) highlight.

steps.text: Check the values carefully. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. When we compare, we subtract: 40 minus 20.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation highlights Pencils (40) and Markers (20), then shows 40 minus 20 equals 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. How many more means compare. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. We subtract: 40 minus 20 equals 20. The answer is 20 more pencils sold than markers. That's what compare means: find the difference.
